back_translation: And the son of Saul heard that Abner had died in Hebron, and his hands grew weak, and all Israel was dismayed.

And the son of Saul had two men who were captains of raiding bands; the name of the one was Baanah, and the name of the second Rechab, sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, of the sons of Benjamin — for Beeroth also is reckoned to Benjamin,
and the Beerothites fled to Gittaim, and have been there as foreigners until this day.

And Jonathan son of Saul had a son, lame of feet; he was five years old when the report concerning Saul and Jonathan came from Jezreel; and his nurse picked him up and fled. And it happened, in her haste to flee, that he fell and became lame. And his name was Mephibosheth.
And the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, Rechab and Baanah, went, and they came in the heat of the day to the house of Ish-bosheth, who was lying down on the bed of midday.
And they came up to the midst of the house as if taking wheat, and they struck him in the belly; and Rechab and Baanah his brother escaped.
And they came into the house while he was lying on his bed in his bedroom, and struck him and killed him, and removed his head; and they took his head and went the way of the Arabah all the night.

And they brought the head of Ish-bosheth to David at Hebron, and said to the king, 'Behold the head of Ish-bosheth son of Saul your enemy, who sought your life; and the LORD has given to my lord the king vengeance this day from Saul and from his seed.'
And David answered Rechab and Baanah his brother, sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, and said to them, 'As the LORD lives, who has redeemed my soul from all distress —
when one told me, saying, Behold, Saul is dead, and he was as a bearer of good news in his own eyes, I seized him and killed him in Ziklag, which was the reward I gave him for his news.
How much more, when wicked men have killed a righteous man in his own house upon his bed — and now shall I not require his blood from your hand, and remove you from the earth?'

And David commanded the young men, and they killed them, and cut off their hands and their feet, and hung them over the pool in Hebron; but the head of Ish-bosheth they took and buried in the tomb of Abner in Hebron.
